title: Characters of nilpotent groups
journal: Mathematical Proceedings of the Cambridge Philosophical Society
abstract: AbstractThe characters (extremal positive definite central functions) of discrete nilpotent groups are studied. The relationship between the set of characters of G and the primitive ideals of the group C*-algebra C*(G) is investigated. It is shown that for a large class of nilpotent groups these objects are in 1–1 correspondence. One proof of this exploits the fact that faithful characters of certain nilpotent groups vanish off the finite conjugacy class subgroup. An example is given where the latter property fails.
